mnemonics: COPY FIRST, WRITE LATER: Remember that a new copy is only made when a process tries to write (modify) the shared page, not when it first reads it.
keyPoints: A memory-management strategy where multiple processes share the same physical memory pages initially.
A private copy of the page is created for a process only when it attempts to modify (write to) the shared page.
It is used to efficiently implement process forking (fork()) by sharing the parent's address space until a write is necessary.
commonMistakes: Assuming a copy is made immediately upon forking a process, rather than only on a write attempt.
Confusing Copy-on-Write with simply sharing memory; the key difference is the creation of a private copy upon modification.
